title: Xanthy Rich Turkish Lamb Pilaf
description: A hearty and cozy Turkish dish featuring lamb, poppy seeds, and a mirepoix base, perfect for a cold winter night. This kosher-friendly recipe is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated carrot and diced celery and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the poppy seeds, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the lamb broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Add the long grain rice and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
